sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient presents with a 4-day history of right elbow pain, located on the medial aspect, which radiates down her arm.
She reports moderate tenderness at the medial epicondyle, pain with resisted pronation of the right forearm, and pain with flexion and bending of the wrist.
She has been taking ibuprofen but has not found relief.
The pain is a 7 out of 10 and interferes with her sleep.
She has a history of hypertension and diabetes, for which she is currently taking lisinopril 10mg daily and metformin 500mg twice a day, respectively.
She has a sweet tooth and enjoys baking, which may be contributing to her blood sugar control issues.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, for which she is currently taking lisinopril 10mg daily.
She also has diabetes, for which she is currently taking metformin 500mg twice a day.
She has a history of being non-adherent with her medication regimen, particularly with her metformin.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Lisinopril 10mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg twice a day for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/70, respiratory rate 18, temperature 97.9°F, oxygen saturation 100%, pulse rate [NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: The patient has moderate tenderness at the medial epicondyle of the right elbow.
Pain is present with resisted pronation of the right forearm and with flexion and bending of the wrist.
The rest of the physical exam is unremarkable.
Test Results:
X-ray of the right elbow: No fracture or bony abnormality is seen, supporting the assessment of medial epicondylitis.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has medial epicondylitis of the right elbow, which is consistent with overuse and damage to the tendons in her arm.
Problem list: Medial epicondylitis of the right elbow; diabetes; hypertension.
Plan:
I prescribed 600mg of ibuprofen every 6 hours with food for 1 week to manage the pain.
I ordered a sling for the right arm to be worn while awake.
I advised the patient to apply ice to the elbow for 20 minutes, 3 times a day.
I increased the patient's metformin to 1000mg twice a day to improve blood sugar control.
I referred the patient to diabetic counseling to discuss steps to improve blood sugar control.
I continued the patient on lisinopril 10mg daily for hypertension.
Follow-up: The patient is to follow up with me in 2 weeks to review her progress and adjust her treatment plan as needed.
